Okay, so the first section here we talk about is the charismatic leader and. Or the attractive character. How many of you guys right now, guys and gals are the attractive character in your business? Most of you guys? How many of you guys are somebody else? That's kind of like the face of it.
So I kind of have a little bit of both. Alright, so this is the key. Every movement has. This has a charismatic leader, attractive character. There's not any of them that don't. Can you think of one? I can apple with Steve Jobs. Christianity's got Christ. There's every single thing. There's one. He's very charismatic, but that's the key essential thing. So it's like, hey, now we know that. What do we know about these people? What can we reverse engineer to figure out for ourselves? And one thing is the biggest thing, as I've been talking about this book and sharing more and more people, I get tons of resistance from people. Like, why didn't. I'm not expert. I don't, you know. And like, there's always that. That thing, right? And I understand that, like the feeling of inadequacy. I don't know if I'm good enough. I don't know if I'm smart enough. I don't know if like, I don't know about you guys, but I still feel that, like, I've been so nervous about this. I put so much time and effort because, like, I'm scared to death to get up. Like, you see me jumping like a little kid back. I'm so excited. I'm so nervous because, like, I want to produce for you guys. I don't want any of you guys going like, man, that was a waste of three days, right? Like, I have the same inadequacies. Like, I'm nervous, like, and everyone has that. But like, and this kind of goes back to what we talked about at the beginning of the book, like, for all of us, like, there's a time where we go through this phase and there's always different phases in life, right? But there's a phase where like, you figured out your thing, your craft, and you got excited, you started studying and you geeked out and you got better and better and better at it. But there's a point where like, it doesn't matter how much better you get. Like, you can't. Like, there's a point you can't keep progressing, right? Like, Tony Robbins, if you've been to his event, he talks about, like, Yeah, I read 700 books in, like, a year. And he's like, now what? I know everything, but now what? And you can't. You physically. You stop your progression. You cannot move from that point until you help somebody else. The first time I ever saw this was that I ever understood. It was my senior year. I wrestled all through high school. I was a state champ. Senior year, took second place in the country. And I was, like, about to go into college, and I was like. I thought I was, like, the coolest guy in the world, right? And my coach said, hey, do you want to come coach at a wrestling camp this summer? I was like, that would be so cool. I think they paid, like, 10 bucks a day. I'm like, dude, I'm going to be rich. I'd have almost 100 bucks at the end of this camp. I was really excited. So we go to this camp, and he's like, hey, teach these kids how to do the things you're good at. So I'm, okay. So I'm teaching these kids and I show them, like, this is the boot. Okay, Go do it. And they do it. I'm like, crap. None of them did it. I'm like, okay, no, do it like this. And they did it. And like, no one could do it. I'm like, dang it. So I'm like, I'm going to do it real quick and watch me. So I did it, and I'm like, what am I doing? I was like, okay, my elbow's here. My hips are here. I'm like, oh, that's why it works. And I find this pattern, right? Just like we're doing now. I find the pattern. I'm like, oh. So it only works if my knee's here, my hips here, and the pressure's tight here. So I showed the kids, okay, you gotta. And I show them that, and all of a sudden, they get it. And I was like, wow. And then I realized, like, that's the pattern. And then I'm like, I didn't know that was happening before. Like, I'd done so many times. I was good at it, but I didn't know the reason. So I got into college. All of a sudden, that summer was huge for me because I learned all the reasons why I was doing things, and I saw the pattern, and I was able to Go back and become better and better. Cause I was like, man, if things aren't working, it's cause I'm missing one of those three or four points. And I became a much better wrestler because of it. Same thing in this business. Like, there was a time where I was just like, you see those who've done the office tour, like, you've seen the library. It's ridiculous. And that's after we threw away an entire dumpster, like probably four or five feet above the dumpster full of courses when we moved here. So that's like what's left over that made it through the trash. And I went through a phase where I was just geeking out on everything. Like I just consumed as much as I could. I was implementing, I was doing some stuff and having fun. But it was like I couldn't keep progressing until there's so much stuff. I just wanted to share it. And then as I started sharing it, that's when I progressed. Right. I'm just going to use you guys as a good example. You guys took all the stuff I'm sharing. You went, and you've changed. How many people's lives have you guys affected now? That is growth for me. I can't do what Kaylin and Brandon, I can't do that. I can't do any of you guys do, right? But the fact I can now share this stuff and you go and you do it and you affect a million people, A million women lose all this weight. Holy crap. That fills me up more than anything that I can do on my own. There's a point where the only way you can progress is by doing that and taking that leap of faith from being a student to being an expert. And so it's scary and it freaks me out. But we all have to be willing to take that leap of faith. And I got a quote for you guys from Garrett White for those who need a little extra pushing and convincing. I actually quoted this in my book and publisher's like, you can't say that. It's not proper English. I'm like, it doesn't matter. It's right. Okay, so listen to this. This is very important.
Alright, can you guys all surrender the fact you're gonna suck for a little bit. Okay. One of the last, like, ignite events we did before we kind of closed that program down, I showed people a video of me. Wasn't that like, four years ago? But anybody here for that event? And it showed me. And I was on camera, and I was so scared I wouldn't make eye contact with the camera. So I'm looking at the ground, and we're trying to do. It was a JV video for one of these products we were promoting or we were trying to sell. I'm looking at the camera, I'm all nervous, and I'm, like, talking, and I wouldn't even dare look at the camera. That's how scared I was. And that was, like, four years ago. And it's funny. Cause, like, now people are like, oh, well, you're so good in front of camera. You're so good on stage. I'm like, are you kidding me? It's because I stood up here like an idiot over and over and over and over again. We'll get to the perfect webinar stuff probably tomorrow. But, like, I didn't learn how to sell by, like, reading a book, then selling. Like, I got on a stage, I walked up there in front of, like, a couple hundred people, I did my thing, I sold, and nobody moved. And I was like, oh, crap, that was embarrassing, huh? All right, well, let's try. Then I walk back off, and next time I come out, okay, please let someone buy. Please let someone buy. Do my thing. And one dude bought. I'm like, why'd you buy? He's like, cause you said this. I'm like, okay, cool. I'll say more of that. And they kept doing it over and over. And I was on the road for three years doing the public speaking circuits two or three times a month, flying somewhere, speaking over and over and over again to learn how to actually do it. I've been telling most of you guys when Periscope first came out, you should do a periscope every single day for the rest of your life. And a bunch of you guys started and then stopped. And the big common theme is, like, well, no one's watching me. It doesn't matter. It's not about the people watching you. It's about you guys, about us learning our voice and understanding that that's the most important thing. The reason why I get on bidding now is because I've done so many videos over and thousands of videos, and that's it. It's just consistent repetition over and over and over. Again, but at first, you're gonna suck. But eventually, you'll suck so little, you'll actually be good, which is the exciting part. But it comes down to being willing to do it, which is why we're gonna be, like, Facebooking live things live from here the next three days, which is gonna be super uncomfortable for some of you guys, but you're gonna do it, and you're gonna talk and you're gonna feel stupid, and you're gonna hate it, and. And some of you guys have people watch it. Some of you guys won't. And it's okay. It doesn't even matter. I always tell people, when I first started my marketing your car podcast, we set up the RSS feed wrong, so we couldn't track stats. So I had no idea how many people were listening until, like, three weeks ago, we finally got it fixed. So for three years, I had no idea. And I am so grateful. I never knew, because if I would have known, like, wow, there's only two people listening. I would have stopped. Now I'm 300 episodes in, and we have this huge following. We finally got the RSS feed figured out, like, three weeks ago. We're already at, like, 400,000 listens in, like, three weeks. I'm like, crap. People are listening. But they weren't at first. But I kept doing it over and over. In fact, Steven told me, he's like, yeah, the first, like, 41 episodes of your stuff, like, they weren't that good, man. But about 42 is like, when I started to actually give some good things, I'm like, oh, cool. I didn't. I don't know. I was just talking in my phone while I was driving. Cause that's what I knew how to do. Okay? So don't be worried about. It's just like, it's consistently doing these things over and over and over again. We had Andrew o' Brien does our PR stuff, was here last weekend at the certified partner event, and then took him to lunch afterwards. And what was cool, like, this comes back to creating offers. But all these things, he's just like, yeah. He's like, I just go. And I create something, and I try to sell it, and nobody buys it, and I create something else. So he's like. He showed me. Over the last six weeks, he launched, like, five or six different programs. He's like, I launched that one nobody bought, so I just didn't do it. Then I launched this program nobody bought, so I didn't do it. Then I launched this one, and. And people Went crazy. We sold 100 of them. And I was like, what's the difference? I just kept making them do things and seeing what people bought, and then they bought that one. So then we went deep and made a whole bunch of things. And then I tried this one nobody bought and this one nobody bought. But the five things I put out there, it's like one thing people bought. So then went and did more of that. It's the same thing with you being an expert, doing Facebook lives or Snapchat. All these different things you're doing, you're putting out there. It's less about making money right now. It's more about finding your voice and getting comfortable in that state and being good with yourself. And then as you're putting things out there, you're gonna find out what people actually resonate with. A good example is Anthony when he launched the Biohacking Secrets book. First off, it wasn't working very good. And so we got on a call. I was like, hey, you need to do a Facebook live a day, every single day till forever. He's like, all right, man. And then I was like, do you remember I told you? I'm like, do all the weird stuff. Get the lights on your head and stuff up your nose. That's gonna go crazy viral. And he did, and nobody cared. But then he did this one. It was like, how to biohack your vegetables. And I was like, dude, that's the stupidest. Put the lights in your nose while you're doing that. But he didn't. And biohacking your vegetables got, like, 3 or 4 million views, and you don't know what people want, so you gotta put out a whole bunch of stuff out there. And he's got, like, I don't know, like, dozens of videos now that have gone crazy viral and even more that have failed. But it's, like, consistency. I think, for now, you're pretty dialed in. Like, you know what people react to and what they don't now. And that's the key, is, like, just consistently doing over and over and over again and being okay feeling stupid when they don't. Because that's the coolest thing, is that nobody knows. Like, if you fail, like, yeah, nobody really knows, which is kind of nice. Only people really know is yourself. But if you don't tell anybody, then nobody knows. In fact, the very first event I ever did, I was at, I joined Matt Fury, had this Mastermind program, so I joined it. And the very first call he was on there, it was so funny. The guy Was like, some guy called, and he's like, I have a question. Like, I'm in the weightlifting market, and I lift weights and, like, how do you guys grow my business? Matt's like, you know what you should do, man? You should put on an event at your house for $5,000, and people will come to you and lift weights in your garage with you. He's like, think people buy that? He's like, oh, yeah. He's like, sweet, man. All right, I'll do that. Next guy gets on. Hey, so I write articles for living, and I submit them, and, like, what should I do? And he's like, you know what you should do? You should an event where you charge $5,000. Have people come to your house, write articles with them, and submit them with them. You guys can get to work. Matt's like, yeah. I was like, huh? And then three people in a row, he told the exact same advice. And so I'm sitting there like, yeah, I'm going to put on an event for $5,000 in my house and see people do it. So that night, send an email out to my list, and I was like, there's this event in my house. It's $5,000. It'd be amazing. And next day, I woke up. Look at my PayPal account. Nobody bought. I'm like, oh, that sucks. Well, nobody knows. And then the next day, two orders came through. I was like, oh, crap. So I made 10 grand. I'm like, oh, my gosh. That was insane for me. I was like, two people bought. And first I was all excited, like, celebrating, and I was like, oh, man. If we go to an event and only two people are there, I can look like an idiot. I was like, but I'm not getting that money back either. So I called everyone I knew, like, my friends. That's why Brent. I called Brent. We just became friends. I'm like, hey, I'm doing this event. Everyone paid five grand. Can you come and not tell anybody about it? That'd be so nice. So he came, my friends came, Everybody I knew came, and I was like, just don't tell those two that you didn't pay, because it's gonna be really awkward. So I did my first event that way, and it was like. It was super ghetto, too. We're in this hotel, and we had the camera set up, and there was windows behind it, and the windows were shining. The camera guy's like, I can't see anything. So we got duct tape, and we were duct taping up blankets from the hotel. Bedroom to cover it. And anyway, it was horrible, but it worked. And people had a great experience and got me to do my first event. And then from there, anyway, everything kind of started. But anyway, it has to do with just trying it. Who knows what's going to work, but being okay with it's probably going to fail because at first you're going to suck, but eventually you're going to suck so little it'll actually be good. That's the plan, at least.
